data.narrative:
Koloma is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Tom Darrah, Pete Johnson, and Paul Harraka that has raised $245M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 peers—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private companies in the Climate & Energy sector at the same stage. Key private comparables include Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $245M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B progression, yielding an implied value of $466M. The peer-median method benchmarks against six same-sector, same-stage comparables, producing a median valuation of $355M (range: $120M–$5.0B). The wide range reflects heterogeneity in the climate-tech sector at this stage.

These methods triangulate to a valuation range of $302M (low) to $535M (high), with a median implied value of $410M. Key uncertainties center on peer selection dispersion—the presence of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B versus peers clustered at $300–500M creates material sensitivity in median estimates. The stage-progression method's reliance on total raised as a proxy for current fair value assumes capital efficiency consistent with peer norms.
